reasoning: After analyzing extensive news coverage from May 7-21, critical findings: (1) India conducted kinetic missile attacks in Pakistan proper during Operation Sindoor (May 7), establishing the precedent and capability. (2) A ceasefire was declared May 10 and appears to be holding through May 21, but described as 'fragile'/'uneasy' with domestic anger in India. (3) The crisis was deemed 'concluded' by some analysts by May 18-19, with military pullback agreed. (4) Historical pattern: India-Pakistan ceasefires often hold after initial violations but can collapse under pressure. (5) Key uncertainty: Domestic political pressure in India to resume stronger action vs. international pressure to maintain ceasefire. Lowering probability from 0.42 to 0.38 given evidence that ceasefire is more stable than initially feared (military pullback talks, crisis deemed 'concluded'), though June breakdown risk remains non-trivial due to inherent fragility and past precedent.
